title: Move locks to shim layer
body:
Previously, the locks were on the provider layer, which doesn't make a vm operation with a config file update atomic. Move them up a layer, so the entire function locks while doing provider and config operations.

This adds a Remove and a Set function to the shim layer.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] Unsure how to test this

comments.body:
This should solve the locking issue in qemu. Currently untested on platforms that are not appleHV, haven't gotten to that yet. 

<sup><sub>please don't comment about me being up late sometimes i drink too much caffeine too late and get hyperfocused at night</sub></sup>
Cockpit tests failed for commit 906392917a7862e054302b138f5586ac624142d4. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 204eabc6c7beda408ba0fb7e4ecd3b33df430173. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

Ugh, looks like the [flake](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6010663953235968/html/machine-linux-podman-fedora-39-aarch64-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html#t--podman-machine-list-list-machine--check-if-running-while-starting--1) is still triggered with this PR. I don't think its right to throw a lock onto `list`, since it makes sense to look at if the machine is in a "starting" state or not. Do you have any ideas, @Luap99 ?

I don't think its right to throw a lock onto `list`, since it makes sense to look at if the machine is in a "starting" state or not. Do you have any ideas, @Luap99 ?

If you rebase the list flake should be fixed with https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21857

Looking at the HyperV deadlock now, trying to make it make sense.
Found it, should be good to review now!
